answers.rationale: Lifecycle CLIs can express later deprecate/split/merge actions, but there is no governed machine-unit unit_design_status field workflow yet. S9 accepts a references/data overlay now and blocks protected machine edits and lifecycle CLI execution inside S9.

answers.decision: Redistribute content to successor elasticity units and retire D04 later through CLI.
answers.rationale: Earlier R2 human review concluded there is no need for a separate D04 unit. Goods classification belongs inside the relevant elasticity units. S9 recommends redistribution then retirement while authorizing no protected mutation.

answers.decision: The proposed successor mapping is complete enough for CP-5.
answers.rationale: A15/D06, A17/D11, and A16/D12/D27 cover the price, income, and cross-elasticity lanes for the decision gate. A later mutation sprint still needs exact operational targets.

answers.decision: The dependent-unit audit is complete enough for CP-5.
answers.rationale: The audit covers D04, A15, A16, A17, D06, D11, D12, D27, target exercise 2.1.3, and exam ha-1022-a-25-2-o question 8. It is sufficient for closure but not for direct mutation.

answers.decision: Keep D04 blocked until later CLI mutation.
answers.rationale: The overlay marks D04 unstable and promotion-blocked, and blocks exercise promotion, diagnostics, adaptive routing, mastery, sequencing, student-facing AI, summative use, PV projection, and PV machine promotion.
S9 resolves the decision gate conditionally. Later CLI-only mutation is required, and S9 must not mutate protected machine references.

pattern_analysis.summary: Answers consistently preserve derived-overlay storage, D04 unit-design framing, promotion blocking, and protected-reference mutation boundaries.
